items.title: This Is Water
items.author: David Foster Wallace
items.description: Kenyon College commencement address (2005). On empathy, consciousness, and the difficulty of living deliberately. ~144 pages. Essential reading on mindfulness and human connection

items.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
items.author: Daniel Kahneman
items.description: System 1 (fast/intuitive) vs System 2 (slow/deliberate) thinking. Synthesis of decades of cognitive bias & heuristics research. National Book Award finalist

items.title: How the Mind Works
items.author: Steven Pinker
items.description: Reverse-engineering human cognition via evolutionary psychology. ~672 pages. Pulitzer Prize finalist. Foundational cognitive science — perception, emotion, language, art, consciousness

items.title: The Alignment Problem
items.author: Brian Christian
items.description: Building AI systems aligned with human values. Based on interviews with leading AI researchers. 3 parts: Prophecy (prediction) / Agency (RL) / Normativity (values). 2022 Schmidt Award

items.title: A Brief History of Intelligence
items.author: Max S. Bennett
items.description: 5 key evolutionary breakthroughs in brain development → implications for building general AI. Academic companion: 'Five breakthroughs' (Front Neuroanatomy 2021). 78 Google Scholar citations

items.title: On Intelligence
items.author: Jeff Hawkins
items.description: Memory-Prediction Framework (HTM). Brain as predictive hierarchy — Hawkins (Palm/Numenta founder) proposes a theory of intelligence. Precursor to Thousand Brains Project
